notes:
Navigation Chrome & Home polish — aligns Home, All Notes and the references
panel to the canonical design (PRD Addendum 22, spec v9.2).

**New**

- **Mentions everywhere.** What used to be called "linked references" is now
  **Mentions** throughout — the right-side panel, the line under a note's title,
  the focused-block view, and the command palette. ("Mentioned in N notes" §8.5.)
- The "Mentioned in N notes" section is now a **clickable bar** you can collapse
  or expand, with sort, a filter funnel, and "show all" (§8.5.1).

**Fixes & polish**

- **Home** now matches the design: a date line under the greeting, an outline
  "New note" button, a highlighted "Pick up where you left off" card, pinned
  notes as a three-up card grid with snippets, and a mention-count badge on each
  recent note (PRD Addendum 22 §6).
- **All Notes** is now a proper table — a live note count, column headers, and
  aligned columns (Title · snippet · Mentions · Updated · Folder · Tags) with the
  whole row clickable (§8.7).
- **All Folders and All Tags** now use the same Browse screen as All Notes
  instead of separate one-off pages, so they look and behave consistently;
  folder create / rename / delete still live there (§9).
- The **references panel and the bottom of a note now show only Mentions** — the
  empty "Todos" and "Unlinked References" sections were removed.
- The **show-sidebar button** in the collapsed layout is now a proper bordered
  button set off from the back/forward controls (§8.3).
